page: Microsoft Azure D16s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv4 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor built on the x86_64 architecture, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 16 dedicated virtual CPUs, which map to 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz and includes 35 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI.

The server is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met using external Premium Storage, which the instance is capable of supporting. There are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration.

Benchmark evaluations place the D16s_v4 in the average performance tier across most metrics. It achieves a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1412.09 and a multi-core score of 14877.24. Memory bandwidth tests show read speeds of 830.85 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 73.63 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, alongside a write speed of 44.64 GB/sec. The instance handles Redis SET operations at 13.9 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 12.09 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, it processes 135M parameter LLM prompts at 1067.89 tokens per second, though performance drops to a weak 24.58 tokens per second for 7B parameter models. This resource profile is suitable for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, database operations, and moderate web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure D16s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs (8 physical cores, 2 threads per core) operating at 2.6 GHz, supported by a 35 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU ratio. It lacks local storage and GPUs, relying entirely on external Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 14877.24, Redis SET performance of 13.9 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput of 12.09 GB/sec. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for balanced workloads, making it suitable for software compilation, database tasks, and general-purpose web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability, delivering balanced performance for standard enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16s_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and Premium Storage support for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and flexible external storage integration for standard enterprise workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Premium Storage capable configuration with no local SSD storage bundled
Average-tier performance across CPU, memory, and database benchmarks
Suitable for general-purpose applications, database operations, and web serving
